verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Belgas Early morning
verdict.reason: Best combined score from the checked sources, with no major blocker for Belgas.

best.windFit: W 5 km/h · glassy · good wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.credit: Photo: Tiago Lima, CC BY 2.0, via Wikimedia Commons

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Surfline is showing surf height at the spot (0.6-0.9 m); Surf-Forecast is open-water swell context (1.2 m @ 11 s). Energy 136 kJ, just enough to be useful, 11 s period, surfable medium power, and consistency 59/100, workable set frequency. The wind state is glassy, so treat it like a premium clean-water signal. The north coast rule is active because Lagide is under 1 m, so exposed beaches can turn on while Baleal looks too small.
best.shortExplanation: 0.6-0.9 m Surfline surf · 136 kJ, just enough to be useful · 11 s period · glassy

ranked.explanation: Surfline is showing surf height at the spot (0.3-0.6 m); Surf-Forecast is open-water swell context (0.1 m @ 13 s). Energy 62 kJ, light/soft, 13 s period, stronger groundswell signal, and consistency 62/100, workable set frequency. Wind matches the spot well at 10 km/h. The north coast rule is active because Lagide is under 1 m, so exposed beaches can turn on while Baleal looks too small.
